title: Refrigerator Ice Maker Not Making Ice
user_phrasings: the ice maker on my whirlpool fridge is not working
my refrigerator stopped making ice
ice maker quit working no cubes
fridge isn't producing ice anymore
ice maker not making ice how do I fix it
barely any ice coming out
whirlpool ice maker won't make ice
content:
When a refrigerator ice maker stops producing ice, work through the most common causes in order:

1. **Freezer temperature** – The freezer must be at or below 0°F (-18°C) for the ice maker to cycle. If it's warmer, ice production slows or stops. Verify the setting and give it 24 hours.
2. **Frozen or kinked water fill tube** – The small tube that delivers water to the ice maker can freeze shut. Thaw it carefully with warm water or a hair dryer on low.
3. **Water inlet valve** – This valve opens to let water into the ice maker. If it's faulty or clogged, no water reaches the tray. This is one of the most common failures.
4. **Ice maker assembly** – If water is reaching the unit but it still won't cycle or eject cubes, the ice maker module or motor may have failed and the whole assembly needs replacing.

After any fix, allow up to 24 hours for the first full batch of ice.

title: Refrigerator Not Cooling Properly
user_phrasings: my fridge isn't getting cold
refrigerator not cooling but freezer is fine
food is spoiling fridge too warm
fridge section warm freezer cold
refrigerator stopped cooling
my whirlpool fridge won't stay cold
everything in the fridge is going bad
content:
A refrigerator that runs but won't cool usually points to one of these:

1. **Evaporator fan motor** – If the fridge is warm but the freezer is still cold, the evaporator fan that moves cold air between compartments may have failed. Listen for the fan; a silent or noisy fan is a strong clue.
2. **Temperature control thermostat** – A failed cold control may stop signaling the compressor to run. 
3. **Frost-covered evaporator coils** – If the defrost system fails, frost builds up on the coils and blocks airflow, so the compartment warms even though the compressor runs.
4. **Condenser coils** – Dirty condenser coils make the system inefficient. Clean them with a coil brush and vacuum.
5. **Door seal** – A worn door gasket lets warm air in and makes the fridge run constantly without staying cold.

title: Frost or Ice Building Up in the Freezer
user_phrasings: frost building up in my freezer
ice on the back wall of the freezer
freezer keeps frosting over
thick ice buildup inside freezer
my ge fridge has frost everywhere
snow like frost in the freezer compartment
content:
Excessive frost in a freezer typically means the automatic defrost system isn't working:

1. **Defrost heater** – This heater melts frost off the evaporator coils several times a day. When it fails, frost accumulates until it blocks airflow and the fridge stops cooling. This is the most common cause.
2. **Defrost thermostat** – It tells the system when the coils are cold enough to need a defrost cycle. A failed thermostat prevents the heater from turning on.
3. **Defrost timer or control board** – Controls the defrost schedule. If it doesn't advance into defrost, frost builds up.
4. **Door seal** – A poor door seal lets humid air in, which freezes into frost. Check the gasket if frost forms near the door.

Manually defrost the unit (unplug for 24 hours) before replacing parts so you start from a clean state.

title: Bad-Tasting Water or Slow Dispenser
user_phrasings: water from my fridge tastes bad
dispenser water comes out slow
filter light is on
funny taste in the ice and water
how often should I change the water filter
water trickles out of the dispenser
my refrigerator water smells weird
content:
Taste, odor, and slow-flow problems at the dispenser almost always trace back to the water filter:

1. **Replace the water filter** – Filters should be changed every six months. An old filter causes bad taste, odor, and slow flow as it clogs. Use the correct filter for your brand (Whirlpool uses everydrop, GE uses MWF, Frigidaire uses PureSource, Samsung uses its DA29 series).
2. **Flush after replacing** – Run 2–4 gallons through the dispenser to clear carbon fines and trapped air, which otherwise cause sputtering and cloudy water.
3. **Check water pressure** – Household supply below ~40 psi can cause slow dispensing even with a new filter.
4. **Reset the filter indicator** – After replacement, reset the status light so it tracks the next interval.

title: Refrigerator Door Not Sealing / Running Constantly
user_phrasings: my fridge door doesn't seal properly
refrigerator runs all the time
condensation forming inside the fridge
door gasket is torn
fridge door won't stay shut
sweating inside the refrigerator
content:
If the door won't seal, the fridge works harder, runs constantly, and may form condensation:

1. **Inspect the door gasket** – Look for tears, flattened spots, or gaps. Test the seal by closing the door on a dollar bill; if it slides out easily, the gasket is weak.
2. **Clean the gasket** – Sticky residue can prevent sealing. Wipe with warm soapy water.
3. **Check door alignment** – A sagging door from worn hinges can prevent a good seal.
4. **Replace the gasket** – If it's cracked or deformed, install a new door gasket. Soak it in warm water first to make it pliable and easier to seat.

title: Cracked or Broken Door Bin / Shelf
user_phrasings: my door bin cracked
refrigerator shelf broke
door shelf won't stay in place
need to replace a broken fridge bin
the bin on the fridge door snapped
shelf on the door keeps falling
content:
Door bins and shelves are simple to replace once you have the right part:

1. **Identify the exact bin** – Bins vary by model and position (upper vs. lower). Match the part to your model number to ensure a correct fit.
2. **Remove the old bin** – Most bins lift straight up and off the door rails. Some have tabs that must be released first.
3. **Install the new bin** – Align it with the rails at the desired height and press down until it clicks.
4. **Confirm it's seated** – Tug gently to make sure it's locked before loading it.

title: Ice Not Dispensing from the Door
user_phrasings: ice won't come out of the dispenser
auger doesn't turn
ice bucket is cracked
dispenser jammed with ice
no ice dispensing even though the bin is full
my ge fridge won't dispense ice
content:
If the ice maker is producing ice but none dispenses:

1. **Check for a clump or jam** – Ice that partially melts and refreezes can jam the bucket and auger. Empty the bucket and break up any clumps.
2. **Inspect the ice bucket and auger** – A cracked bucket or stripped auger gear will spin without moving ice. Replace the bucket assembly if the drive gear is damaged.
3. **Dispenser motor or switch** – If the auger never turns and there's no hum, the dispenser motor or door switch may have failed.
4. **Avoid long storage** – Ice left unused for weeks tends to clump; using ice regularly keeps it from fusing.

title: Dishwasher Won't Drain / Standing Water
user_phrasings: my dishwasher won't drain
standing water in the bottom of the dishwasher
water left in the dishwasher after the cycle
dishwasher not draining all the way
puddle at the bottom of my whirlpool dishwasher
dirty water sitting in the tub
content:
Standing water after a cycle means water isn't being pumped out:

1. **Clean the filter** – A clogged filter at the bottom of the tub is the most common and easiest cause. Remove and rinse it.
2. **Check for a drain hose clog or kink** – Food debris can block the drain hose, or it can be kinked behind the unit.
3. **Garbage disposal knockout plug** – If a disposal was recently installed, the knockout plug may not have been removed, blocking the drain.
4. **Drain pump** – If the filter and hose are clear, the drain pump may have failed or be jammed with debris (glass, a seed pit). Listen for a humming pump that isn't moving water.

title: Dishes Not Coming Out Clean
user_phrasings: my dishes come out dirty
dishwasher isn't cleaning well
food residue left on dishes
spray arm doesn't seem to spin
plates still have stuck on food after washing
dishwasher leaving grit on glasses
not getting dishes clean anymore
content:
Poor cleaning usually comes down to water not reaching the dishes properly:

1. **Clean the filter** – A dirty filter recirculates food particles back onto dishes. Clean it regularly.
2. **Check the spray arms** – Spin them by hand; they must rotate freely. Clear clogged spray holes with a toothpick. A cracked spray arm won't build pressure.
3. **Don't block the arms** – Tall items can stop the arms from spinning. Reload and retest.
4. **Water temperature** – Water should reach about 120°F. If the heating element has failed, wash water may be too cool to dissolve grease.
5. **Circulation pump** – If arms are clear but cleaning is still poor and the unit is loud, the circulation pump may be failing.

title: Dishwasher Leaking Water
user_phrasings: my dishwasher is leaking
water leaking from the door
puddle on the floor in front of the dishwasher
leak coming from under the dishwasher
door seal looks worn and it's leaking
water on the kitchen floor after running the dishwasher
content:
Track down a leak by where the water appears:

1. **Door seal / gasket** – Leaks at the front usually mean a worn or dislodged door gasket. Inspect it for cracks or flattened areas and replace if needed.
2. **Drain hose connections** – Leaks underneath often come from a cracked drain hose or loose clamps at the pump or drain.
3. **Overfilling** – A stuck water inlet valve can overfill the tub and cause overflow. 
4. **Spray arm seal / pump housing** – Less common, but a cracked pump housing or sump gasket can leak from below.
5. **Level the unit** – A dishwasher that isn't level can pool water toward one corner and leak from the door.

title: Dishwasher Won't Start
user_phrasings: my dishwasher won't start
dishwasher is dead no power
door won't latch so it won't run
press start and nothing happens
bosch dishwasher won't turn on
no lights on the dishwasher
content:
When a dishwasher won't start at all:

1. **Door latch** – The door must latch fully for the cycle to begin. If the latch is broken or misaligned, the unit thinks the door is open and won't start. This is a very common cause.
2. **Power** – Confirm the dishwasher has power: check the breaker and the cord/junction box connection under the sink.
3. **Control panel buttons** – Unresponsive buttons can indicate a failed control board or touchpad.
4. **Child lock** – Make sure the control lock / child lock feature isn't enabled.
5. **Thermal fuse** – A blown thermal fuse on the control board cuts power to the unit.

title: Dishes Not Drying / Staying Wet
user_phrasings: my dishes are still wet at the end
dishwasher isn't drying the dishes
everything is wet after the cycle
no heat drying anymore
dishes come out soaked
plastic items stay wet in the dishwasher
content:
Wet dishes after a cycle point to the drying system or rinse aid:

1. **Rinse aid** – Low or empty rinse aid is the #1 cause of poor drying; it helps water sheet off dishes. Refill the dispenser.
2. **Heating element** – On units with heated dry, a failed heating element leaves dishes wet (and may also leave wash water too cool). Test it for continuity.
3. **Unload bottom-up** – Water pooled on upturned items can spill onto lower dishes when unloading top-first.
4. **High-temp / heated-dry setting** – Make sure a heated dry or sanitize option is selected.
5. **Plastic items** – Plastics hold water and dry poorly even on a healthy machine; this can be normal.

title: Dishwasher Won't Fill with Water
user_phrasings: my dishwasher isn't filling with water
no water going into the dishwasher
dishwasher fills very slowly
not enough water in the tub
I can hear it running but no water comes in
content:
If the dishwasher runs but doesn't fill:

1. **Water supply** – Confirm the water shutoff valve under the sink is fully open.
2. **Water inlet valve** – This valve lets water into the tub. If it's clogged with sediment or has failed electrically, little or no water enters. This is the most common cause of a no-fill.
3. **Float switch** – A stuck float can falsely tell the control the tub is full, stopping the fill. Check that the float moves freely.
4. **Inlet screen** – Sediment can clog the screen on the inlet valve; cleaning it can restore flow.

title: Dish Rack Won't Roll or Stay Level
user_phrasings: my dishwasher rack won't roll smoothly
upper rack keeps falling down
rack came off the track
rack wheel broke
lower rack drags and tips
the top rack won't stay level
content:
Rack problems are mechanical and usually quick to fix:

1. **Rack rollers / wheels** – Worn or broken rollers make the rack drag, tip, or jump the track. Replace the affected wheels or roller kit.
2. **Upper rack adjusters** – If the upper rack sags or falls, the adjuster mechanism on one or both sides has likely broken. Replace the adjuster kit (left and right).
3. **Rack stops / end caps** – Missing end caps let the rack roll all the way off the rails. Replace them to keep the rack captured.
4. **Realign the rails** – Make sure the rails are seated correctly before installing new parts.

title: Dishwasher Smells Bad
user_phrasings: my dishwasher smells bad
musty smell from the dishwasher
rotten food odor in the dishwasher
stinky dishwasher even after running
bad smell when I open the dishwasher
dishwasher has a funky odor
content:
Odors come from trapped food and standing moisture:

1. **Clean the filter** – A clogged filter is the most common source of smell. Remove it and wash it in warm soapy water.
2. **Wipe the door and gasket** – Food and grime collect in the door edges and gasket folds. Clean them with a damp cloth.
3. **Run a cleaning cycle** – Run an empty hot cycle with a dishwasher cleaner or a cup of white vinegar on the top rack.
4. **Check the drain** – Lingering standing water (a partial drain clog) breeds odor; clear any drain blockage.
5. **Spray arms** – Clean clogged spray-arm holes so the tub rinses fully.
